intent: E97-S04 New-device download overlay — symmetric counterpart to E97-S03 upload wizard. Adds useDownloadStatusStore (phase machine), observedHydrate wrapper, useDownloadProgress (HEAD-count snapshot + Dexie poll), useDownloadEngineWatcher (passive engine observer), shouldShowDownloadOverlay predicate, NewDeviceDownloadOverlay component, and App.tsx mount wiring with 2s deferred mount.

findings.title: Progress hook counts rows without userId as current user; predicate excludes them — inconsistent user scoping
findings.why_it_matters: `localCountAll` returns `true` for rows lacking a userId field (count them in), while `shouldShowDownloadOverlay.localHasData` returns `false` for the same rows (exclude them). A brand-new device that still has residual singleton rows (e.g. `notificationPreferences` with id='singleton') from a prior session would pass the emptiness gate, but those rows would then be counted toward `processed`, potentially inflating the processed total. Also inconsistent: if remote HEAD counts scope via `.eq('user_id', userId)` then processed should likewise only count rows belonging to this user.
evidence: useDownloadProgress.ts:110 — `if (rowUserId === undefined || rowUserId === null || rowUserId === '') { return true }`
shouldShowDownloadOverlay.ts:78 — `if (rowUserId === undefined || rowUserId === null || rowUserId === '') { return false }`
Plan Unit 4a mentions singleton tables should be excluded from localHasData (F3 fix) but the progress hook does not mirror that exclusion.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) apply the same singleton-exclusion + userId-only filter in `localCountAll` as in `localHasData`, or (b) document the asymmetry with a code comment explaining why processed count is permissive (e.g., echo-loop of engine writes may land rows with null userId before backfill stamps them).

findings.title: remoteHasAnyRows assumes every synced Supabase table has a user_id column
findings.why_it_matters: Both `remoteHasAnyRows` and `useDownloadProgress.headCount` call `.eq('user_id', userId)` for every entry in `tableRegistry.filter(!skipSync && !uploadOnly)`. If any synced table uses a different foreign-key column (e.g. `owner_id`, `profile_id`), the Supabase query rejects with a column-not-found error. In predicate context this falls through to the safe default (false/unknown), but in the progress hook it increments `totalsFailedCount`, and if enough tables use non-standard names, may silently tip the hook into ALL-HEAD-fail with a misleading 'check your connection' error.
evidence: No lookup against tableRegistry's fieldMap to resolve the actual user-scoped column name per table.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `entry.fieldMap` to resolve the user-scoped column name per table (fall back to `user_id` if unmapped). Alternatively, add a schema-drift test that asserts every !skipSync && !uploadOnly entry exposes a user_id column in Supabase, locking the invariant.

findings.title: Auto-close effect depends on inline `onClose` prop — timer resets on every parent re-render
findings.why_it_matters: The 250ms success→onClose effect includes `onClose` in its dep array. App.tsx passes a fresh inline arrow `() => { setDownloadOverlayUserId(null); setDeferredOverlayReady(false); }` on every render. Any unrelated App re-render during the success window clears + re-arms the 250ms timer. With a stable store status the net effect is still a close within ~250ms, but this couples close latency to App render cadence and is a latent source of test flake under fake timers.
evidence: NewDeviceDownloadOverlay.tsx:156 — `}, [open, storeStatus, progress.done, progress.total, progress.processed, onClose])`
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the overlay's onClose in `useCallback` on the App side, or stash onClose in a ref inside the overlay and drop it from the effect deps. Same pattern applies to `handleRetry` if it ever becomes a dependency.

findings.title: Watchdog + success effects both use window.setTimeout without feature-detecting test env
findings.why_it_matters: The component uses `window.setTimeout`/`window.clearTimeout` with the browser-typed return (`number`). In jsdom with vitest fake timers this works, but mocking cadence for the 60s watchdog in unit tests is expensive (60_000ms advance). Extracting WATCHDOG_MS to an injectable constant or an optional prop would let tests exercise the watchdog deterministically without advancing 60s of fake time.
evidence: Plan Unit 5 test scenarios include `60s watchdog fires from hydrating-p3p4` and from `downloading-p0p2` — each requires 60s of fake-timer advance.
findings.suggested_fix: Allow WATCHDOG_MS and SUCCESS_CLOSE_DELAY_MS to be overridden via an optional prop (default to the production values). Tests pass a shorter watchdog (e.g. 100ms) to exercise the transition.

findings.title: Engine-watcher operates while storeStatus is 'hydrating-p3p4' — scope broader than plan specifies
findings.why_it_matters: Plan §Unit 4a says the watcher 'subscribes only while enabled and downloadStatus === downloading-p0p2'. The implementation also allows the callback to run while status is `hydrating-p3p4` (line 91). `tryCompleteIfQueueDrained` defensively gates on `downloading-p0p2` before calling `completeDownloading`, but the engine-error branch calls `failDownloading` while still in `hydrating-p3p4`, which would override a hydrator-in-progress with an engine-error message. If the engine transitions to `error` during Phase A (before observedHydrate resolves), the overlay jumps to error even if the hydrator is about to succeed.
evidence: Line 111-120 — engine 'error' surfaces failDownloading even when active === 'hydrating-p3p4'
Plan line 487 — 'Subscribes to useSyncStatusStore only while enabled + store phase is downloading-p0p2'
findings.suggested_fix: Either tighten the guard to match the plan (Phase B only) and drop the `hydrating-p3p4` allowance, or update the plan doc to reflect the actual Phase A + Phase B scope. If keeping the broader scope, add a comment explaining why engine errors during Phase A should surface before the hydrator completes.

findings.title: Dev/test escape hatch `__mockHeadCounts` is a single scalar — cannot simulate per-table or partial-failure scenarios
findings.why_it_matters: `window.__mockHeadCounts` returns the same scalar count for every table. Unit 7 plan specifies tests 3b (ALL HEAD fail) and 3c (PARTIAL HEAD fail) — neither can be exercised via this hook because a scalar cannot distinguish tables. The E2E spec (story-97-04.spec.ts) drives the overlay via store shims instead, bypassing the progress hook entirely, so the ALL-vs-PARTIAL branches are tested only in unit tests using vi.mock.
evidence: useDownloadProgress.ts:80-82 — `const mock = (window as any).__mockHeadCounts as number | undefined; if (typeof mock === 'number') return mock;`
Plan Unit 7 §Test 3b and §Test 3c explicitly call for per-table HEAD intercepts, not available through the current shim.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the shim to accept `{ [supabaseTable]: number | 'error' }` so tests can drive per-table success/failure combinations. Alternatively, add a companion `__mockHeadFailures: string[]` listing tables that should throw.

findings.title: observedHydrate throws after setting error state; caller's .catch logs but store error is already set — potential duplicate error surfacing
findings.why_it_matters: `observedHydrate` calls `failDownloading(message)` and then re-throws. The caller in useAuthLifecycle catches and console.errors. On retry via `handleRetry`, the hydrator re-runs; a second failure path walks through `failDownloading` again, overwriting any intermediate state. If the retry's hydrator throws synchronously before the overlay's `startHydrating()` reset (line 163) — unlikely but possible — the store could stay in `error` while a new retry is in flight.
evidence: NewDeviceDownloadOverlay.tsx:160-170 — handleRetry calls startHydrating first, then observedHydrate (ordering correct; this is defensive note only).
findings.suggested_fix: No immediate action required — the retry ordering is correct. Consider adding a unit test that asserts rapid consecutive retries do not leave the store stuck in error.

residual_risks: Plan §R2 counts derived from tableRegistry assume every !skipSync && !uploadOnly Supabase table has a user_id column — not asserted anywhere.

If the engine's initial 'synced' state precedes any user-observable 'syncing' event after sign-in (e.g., user with zero P0-P2 rows to fetch), the fast-path in useDownloadEngineWatcher is the only detection. Covered by code but fragile to future engine changes.
Auto-close effect's onClose-in-deps pattern couples close latency to App render cadence; not observable today but could flake under future App-level re-render churn.
testing_gaps: Plan Unit 7 Tests 3b (ALL HEAD fail) and 3c (PARTIAL HEAD fail) are specified but the E2E spec uses store-driven shims only; the HEAD-count branches are covered in unit tests (useDownloadProgress.test.ts) but not in E2E.
60s watchdog transition — unit test exists but requires full fake-timer advance; consider injecting WATCHDOG_MS.

No E2E assertion that useSyncableWrite is NOT called during the overlay lifetime (echo-loop invariant). Plan Unit 2 test scenario calls this out, and the shouldShowDownloadOverlay test may cover it, but a belt-and-suspenders invariant test at the App level would reinforce it.
learnings: E97-S03 F2 snapshot-before-async race pattern — mirrored correctly in useDownloadProgress via totalRef/snapshotTakenRef.
E96-S02 echo-loop guard (bulkPut, never syncableWrite) — observed; observedHydrate does not write to syncQueue.
Singleton-table exclusion fix (F3 in plan) applied in shouldShowDownloadOverlay.localHasData but asymmetric vs useDownloadProgress.localCountAll — see F1 finding.
schema_drift: N/A — no migration files in diff.
deployment_notes: Client-only UI. No rollout risk. Verify __downloadStatusStore shim is tree-shaken in production (import.meta.env.PROD guard present).
verdict_reasoning: All requirements R1-R6 are addressed in code. No P0/P1 findings. Two P2 semantic concerns (user-scoping asymmetry F1, user_id column assumption F2) are worth addressing before enabling on heterogeneous schema but do not block this feature shipping given safe-default fallbacks. Remaining P3 findings are polish/advisory.
